title: A quick start guide to rTrafa
text: Trafa publishes dozens statistical products. Start listing : Use product_search() filter text:
code: products <- get_products() dplyr::glimpse(products) #> Rows: 56 #> Columns: 5 #> $ name        <chr> "t10011", "t10014", "t10015", "t10017", "t10018", "t10019"… #> $ label       <chr> "Bussar", "Motorcyklar", "Mopeder klass I", "Släpvagnar", … #> $ description <chr> "", "", "", "", "", "", "", "", "Bussar\n\nDefinitioner-> … #> $ id          <int> 203, 206, 207, 209, 210, 211, 222, 227, 231, 232, 233, 234… #> $ active_from <chr> "2019-01-10T10:17:00", "2019-01-10T10:25:00", "2019-01-10T… products |>   product_search("Bussar") |>   product_describe(max_n = 3) #> ── t10011: Bussar ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Active from: 2019-01-10T10:17:00  #>  #> ── t10091: Bussar ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Description: Bussar #>  #> Definitioner-> Bussklass: För fordon som är inrättade för befordran av fler än 22 passagerare utöver föraren finns följande fordonsklasser: Klass I – Fordon som tillverkats med utrymmen för ståplatspassagerare för att medge frekventa förflyttningar av passagerare. Klass II – Fordon som huvudsakligen tillverkats för befordran av sittplatspassagerare och som är utformade för att medge befordran av ståplatspassagerare i mittgången och/eller i ett utrymme som inte är större än att det utrymme som upptas för två dubbelsäten. Klass III – Fordon som uteslutande tillverkats för befordran av sittplatspassagerare. För fordon som är inrättande för befordran av högst 22 passagerare utöver föraren finns följande fordonsklasser: Klass A – Fordon utformade för befordran av ståplatspassagerare. Ett fordon i denna klass är utrustat med säten och ska ha utrymme för ståplatspassagerare. Klass B – Fordon som inte är utformade för befordran av ståplatspassagerare. Ett fordon i denna klass saknar utrymme för ståplatspassagerare. #>   Active from: 2019-04-29T11:00:00  #>  #> ── t10021: Bussar ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Active from: 2020-05-08T12:59:00

title: A quick start guide to rTrafa
text: product several measures (KPIs). Let’s see ’s available Buses (t10011):
code: measures <- get_measures("t10011") measures |> measure_describe() #> ── itrfslut (Antal i trafik) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser i slutet av perioden  #>  #> ── avstslut (Antal avställda) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser i slutet av perioden  #>  #> ── nyregunder (Antal nyregistreringar) ──────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser under perioden  #>  #> ── avregunder (Antal avregistreringar) ──────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser under perioden

title: A quick start guide to rTrafa
text: Dimensions variables can filter . Use get_dimensions() discover : Notice dimensions belong hierarchy (shown brackets). grouped conceptually queried independently. can inspect available values dimension: Note type column: "filter" entries like senaste (latest) forra (previous) shortcuts API resolves dynamically.
code: dims <- get_dimensions("t10011") dims |> dimension_describe() #> ── ar (År) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Data type: Time #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (25): 2001 = 2001, 2002 = 2002, 2003 = 2003, 2004 = 2004, 2005 = 2005 ... and 20 more #>   Filters: senaste = Senaste, forra = Föregående #>  #> ── avregform (Avregistreringsorsak) ─────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 20 = Utförda ur landet, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── dimpo (Direkt import) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 10 = Direkt import, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── leasing (Leasing) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 30 = Leasade, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── bussklass (Bussklass) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Description: Bussklasser enligt föreskrift nr 107 UNECE #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (7): 1 = A, 2 = B, 3 = I, 4 = II, 5 = III ... and 2 more #>  #> ── drivm (Drivmedel) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (10): 101 = Bensin, 102 = Diesel, 103 = El, 104 = Elhybrid, 105 = Laddhybrid ... and 5 more #>  #> ── pass (Antal passagerare) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (12): 101 = – 20, 102 = 21 – 40, 103 = 41 – 50, 104 = 51 – 60, 105 = 61 – 70 ... and 7 more #>  #> ── agarkat (Ägarkategori)  [agare] ──────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (3): 10 = Fysisk person, 20 = Juridisk person, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── tillst (Tillstånd)  [agare] ──────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (3): 1 = Yrkesmässig trafik, 2 = Firmabilstrafik, t1 = Totalt ar_vals <- dimension_values(dims, "ar") ar_vals #> # A tibble: 27 × 3 #>    name    label      type   #>    <chr>   <chr>      <chr>  #>  1 senaste Senaste    filter #>  2 forra   Föregående filter #>  3 2001    2001       value  #>  4 2002    2002       value  #>  5 2003    2003       value  #>  6 2004    2004       value  #>  7 2005    2005       value  #>  8 2006    2006       value  #>  9 2007    2007       value  #> 10 2008    2008       value  #> # ℹ 17 more rows

title: A quick start guide to rTrafa
text: Now let’s fetch data. request number buses traffic last 10 years:

title: A quick start guide to rTrafa
text: Note convert ar column proper Date plotting. Trafa returns ar character column ("2016", "2017", …), plotting integer can produce awkward ggplot2 breaks like 2020, 2022.5, 2025. Wrapping .Date(paste0(ar, "-01-01")) lets scale_x_date() place tick marks whole years — pattern worth reusing time-series analysis.
code: library("ggplot2")  bus_plot <- bus_data |>   dplyr::mutate(year = as.Date(paste0(ar, "-01-01")))  ggplot(bus_plot, aes(x = year, y = itrfslut)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title = "Buses in traffic in Sweden",     x = "Year",     y = "Number of vehicles",     caption = data_legend(bus_data)   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: A quick start guide to rTrafa
text: Deeper walkthrough — vignette("introduction--rtrafa") covers full data model, hierarchies, filter shortcuts, dimension validation. Filter shortcuts — Use ar = "senaste" always get latest year without hardcoding.

title: A quick start guide to rTrafa
text: rTrafa part family R packages Swedish Nordic open statistics share design philosophy: rKolada — R client Kolada database Swedish municipal regional Key Performance Indicators pixieweb — R client PX-Web APIs (Statistics Sweden, Statistics Norway, Statistics Finland, )

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: Trafa API organises Swedish transport statistics four-level hierarchy: Product — statistical domain, e.g. Buses (t10011) Passenger cars (t10016). Think family related statistics. Measure — specific KPI within product. Buses four measures: vehicles traffic, deregistered, newly registered, deregistrations. Dimension — filterable variable year, fuel type, owner category. dimension discrete values can select. Value — individual codes within dimension (e.g. "2024", "102" Diesel). also grouping concept called Hierarchy. hierarchy like “Owner” groups related dimensions (“Owner category” “Licence type”) together. Hierarchies purely organisational — used directly queries. child dimensions appear get_dimensions() hierarchy name noted hierarchy column. rTrafa provides function family first three levels:

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: product table one row per statistical product: entity tables support set operations. product_search() filters regex: product_extract_ids() extracts name column character vector — useful passing downstream functions:
code: products <- get_products() dplyr::glimpse(products) #> Rows: 56 #> Columns: 5 #> $ name        <chr> "t10011", "t10014", "t10015", "t10017", "t10018", "t10019"… #> $ label       <chr> "Bussar", "Motorcyklar", "Mopeder klass I", "Släpvagnar", … #> $ description <chr> "", "", "", "", "", "", "", "", "Bussar\n\nDefinitioner-> … #> $ id          <int> 203, 206, 207, 209, 210, 211, 222, 227, 231, 232, 233, 234… #> $ active_from <chr> "2019-01-10T10:17:00", "2019-01-10T10:25:00", "2019-01-10T… products |>   product_search("Personbilar") |>   product_describe(max_n = 2) #> ── t10094: Personbilar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Description: Personbilar #>  #> Definitioner->Enskild näringsidkare: En enskild näringsidkare är en person som själv driver och ansvarar för ett företag. Enligt bolagsverket är en enskild näringsidkare inte en juridisk person. I fordonsregistret redovisas alla bolagsformer under juridisk person. #>   Active from: 2019-04-29T11:00:00  #>  #> ── t10026: Personbilar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Active from: 2020-05-08T12:59:00  #>  #> ... and 2 more product(s). products |>   product_search("Personbilar") |>   product_extract_ids() #> [1] "t10094" "t10026" "t10036" "t10016"

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: product contains one measures. measure statistic observed — example, Antal trafik (vehicles traffic) Antal nyregistreringar (new registrations). Extract names use get_data():
code: measures <- get_measures("t10011") measures |> measure_describe() #> ── itrfslut (Antal i trafik) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser i slutet av perioden  #>  #> ── avstslut (Antal avställda) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser i slutet av perioden  #>  #> ── nyregunder (Antal nyregistreringar) ──────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser under perioden  #>  #> ── avregunder (Antal avregistreringar) ──────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Product: t10011 #>   Description: Avser under perioden measures |> measure_extract_names() #> [1] "itrfslut"   "avstslut"   "nyregunder" "avregunder"

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: Dimensions filter variables available product. Use get_dimensions() discover :
code: dims <- get_dimensions("t10011") dims |> dimension_describe() #> ── ar (År) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Data type: Time #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (25): 2001 = 2001, 2002 = 2002, 2003 = 2003, 2004 = 2004, 2005 = 2005 ... and 20 more #>   Filters: senaste = Senaste, forra = Föregående #>  #> ── avregform (Avregistreringsorsak) ─────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 20 = Utförda ur landet, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── dimpo (Direkt import) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 10 = Direkt import, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── leasing (Leasing) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 30 = Leasade, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── bussklass (Bussklass) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Description: Bussklasser enligt föreskrift nr 107 UNECE #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (7): 1 = A, 2 = B, 3 = I, 4 = II, 5 = III ... and 2 more #>  #> ── drivm (Drivmedel) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (10): 101 = Bensin, 102 = Diesel, 103 = El, 104 = Elhybrid, 105 = Laddhybrid ... and 5 more #>  #> ── pass (Antal passagerare) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (12): 101 = – 20, 102 = 21 – 40, 103 = 41 – 50, 104 = 51 – 60, 105 = 61 – 70 ... and 7 more #>  #> ── agarkat (Ägarkategori)  [agare] ──────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (3): 10 = Fysisk person, 20 = Juridisk person, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── tillst (Tillstånd)  [agare] ──────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (3): 1 = Yrkesmässig trafik, 2 = Firmabilstrafik, t1 = Totalt

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: dimensions grouped hierarchy. output , dimensions marked [agare] belong “Owner” hierarchy. metadata helps understand logical structure — building query, use child dimension names directly (e.g. agarkat, agare).
code: dims |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(hierarchy)) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   product name    label data_type option description hierarchy n_values values   #>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr>     <lgl>  <chr>       <chr>        <int> <list>   #> 1 t10011  agarkat Ägar… String    TRUE   ""          agare            3 <tibble> #> 2 t10011  tillst  Till… String    TRUE   ""          agare            3 <tibble>

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: dimension set valid values. dimension_values() returns tibble: Note type column. Entries type = "filter" filter shortcuts — dynamic values API resolves query time: senaste — recent available value forra — previous value useful building queries stay current without hardcoding years: Regular dimension values (type = "value") can inspected :
code: ar_vals <- dimension_values(dims, "ar") ar_vals #> # A tibble: 27 × 3 #>    name    label      type   #>    <chr>   <chr>      <chr>  #>  1 senaste Senaste    filter #>  2 forra   Föregående filter #>  3 2001    2001       value  #>  4 2002    2002       value  #>  5 2003    2003       value  #>  6 2004    2004       value  #>  7 2005    2005       value  #>  8 2006    2006       value  #>  9 2007    2007       value  #> 10 2008    2008       value  #> # ℹ 17 more rows # Always gets the latest year, regardless of when you run it get_data("t10011", "itrfslut", ar = "senaste") drivm_vals <- dimension_values(dims, "drivm") drivm_vals #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    name  label      type  #>    <chr> <chr>      <chr> #>  1 101   Bensin     value #>  2 102   Diesel     value #>  3 103   El         value #>  4 104   Elhybrid   value #>  5 105   Laddhybrid value #>  6 106   Etanol     value #>  7 107   Gas        value #>  8 108   Biodiesel  value #>  9 109   Övriga     value #> 10 t1    Totalt     value

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: know measure want, get_dimensions() can validate dimensions compatible: can also pass several measures . API returns intersection — dimensions valid every measure vector. useful want compare combine measures need know filters can apply uniformly: Buses, ar (year) survives intersection — dimensions specific one measure .
code: # Only dimensions valid for the "itrfslut" measure get_dimensions("t10011", measure = "itrfslut")  # See all dimensions with their validity status get_dimensions("t10011", measure = "itrfslut", only_valid = FALSE) # Dimensions valid for BOTH "vehicles in traffic" and "new registrations" get_dimensions("t10011", measure = c("itrfslut", "nyregunder"))

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: get_data() core function. Provide product, measure, optional dimension filters: simplify = TRUE (default), dimension gets code column _label column human-readable name.

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: complex queries, prepare_query() validates selections hitting data endpoint:
code: q <- prepare_query("t10011", "itrfslut",   ar = c("2023", "2024"),   drivm = c("101", "102", "103") ) q #> Trafa query: t10011 | itrfslut #>   Dimension filters: #>     ar = c("2023", "2024") #>     drivm = c("101", "102", "103") #>   Available (unfiltered) dimensions: ...  # Pass the validated query to get_data() get_data(query = q)

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: data_minimize() removes columns values identical — useful filtering single year fuel type: data_legend() generates source caption plots:
code: get_data("t10011", "itrfslut", ar = "2024") |>   data_minimize() data_legend(bus_data) #> [1] "Källa: Trafa; produkt: Bussar (t10011); mått: Antal i trafik (itrfslut)"

what: Example: buses in traffic over time
title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: Note convert ar column proper Date plotting. Trafa returns ar character column ("2016", "2017", …), plotting integer can produce awkward ggplot2 breaks like 2020, 2022.5, 2025. Wrapping .Date(paste0(ar, "-01-01")) lets scale_x_date() place tick marks whole years. pattern ’ll want reuse time-series analysis — rTrafa sibling packages rKolada pixieweb.
code: library("ggplot2")  bus_plot <- bus_data |>   dplyr::mutate(year = as.Date(paste0(ar, "-01-01")))  ggplot(bus_plot, aes(x = year, y = itrfslut)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   # Years as dates, one tick per year   scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title = "Buses in traffic in Sweden",     x = "Year",     y = "Number of vehicles",     caption = data_legend(bus_data)   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Introduction to rTrafa
code: drivm_data <- get_data("t10011", "itrfslut",   ar = "senaste",   drivm = c("101", "102", "103", "104", "105", "106", "107") ) ggplot(drivm_data, aes(   x = reorder(drivm_label, -itrfslut),   y = itrfslut )) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title = "Buses in traffic by fuel type",     subtitle = paste("Year:", drivm_data$ar_label[1]),     x = NULL,     y = "Number of vehicles",     caption = data_legend(drivm_data)   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))

title: Introduction to rTrafa
text: fetching measure different products can compare across vehicle types:
code: pbilar_data <- get_data("t10016", "itrfslut",   ar = as.character(2016:2025) ) comparison <- dplyr::bind_rows(   bus_data |> dplyr::mutate(vehicle = "Buses"),   pbilar_data |> dplyr::mutate(vehicle = "Passenger cars") ) |>   dplyr::mutate(year = as.Date(paste0(ar, "-01-01")))  ggplot(comparison, aes(x = year, y = itrfslut, color = vehicle)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title = "Vehicles in traffic in Sweden",     x = "Year",     y = "Number of vehicles",     color = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "top")

what: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
text: rTrafa R package discovering, inspecting downloading Swedish transport statistics Trafa API — open database maintained Trafikanalys, Sweden’s agency transport analysis. covers road, rail, sea air transport; vehicles traffic; new registrations; passenger freight volumes; . learn using rTrafa, recommended use following resources order:

title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
text: get running quickly rTrafa, please see vignette quick start guide rTrafa. introduction rTrafa design principles functions included, please see Introduction rTrafa. See Reference section package homepage learn full set functionality included package. rTrafa open source licensed Affero Gnu Public License version 3. means free download source, modify redistribute please, copies modifications must retain original license. Please see file LICENSE.md information.

title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
text: rTrafa CRAN. install , run following code R: install latest development version GitHub, use remotes package:
code: install.packages("rTrafa") library("remotes") remotes::install_github("LCHansson/rTrafa")

title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
code: library(rTrafa)  # List the available statistical products products <- get_products()  # Inspect the measures available for "Buses" (product t10011) measures <- get_measures("t10011")  # Download "Vehicles in traffic" for the last ten years bus_data <- get_data("t10011", "itrfslut",   ar = as.character(2016:2025) )

title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
text: Search--fetch workflow: Discover products, inspect measures dimensions, download data Three-level data model: Products → measures → dimensions, mirrored get_products(), get_measures() get_dimensions() Filter shortcuts: Use ar = "senaste" latest period without hardcoding years Dimension validation: prepare_query() checks filters compatible selected measure hitting data endpoint Offline-safe examples: trafa_available() guards network calls examples tests degrade gracefully API

title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
text: multi-user web applications workflows benefit shared, persistent cache, rTrafa integrates nordstatExtras package. installed, get_data(), get_products(), functions can write shared SQLite file instead per-session .rds files: Features include cell-level deduplication across overlapping queries, cross-query freshness propagation, FTS5-powered typeahead search via nxt_search(). See nordstatExtras README details.
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("LCHansson/nordstatExtras")  library(nordstatExtras) handle <- nxt_open("cache.sqlite")  # Data and metadata are cached in the same SQLite file products <- get_products(cache = TRUE, cache_location = handle) bus_data <- get_data("t10011", "itrfslut",   ar = as.character(2020:2025),   cache = TRUE, cache_location = handle )  nxt_close(handle)

title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
text: rTrafa part family R packages Swedish Nordic open statistics share design philosophy — tibble-based, pipe-friendly, offline-safe: rKolada — R client Kolada database Swedish municipal regional Key Performance Indicators pixieweb — R client PX-Web APIs (Statistics Sweden, Statistics Norway, Statistics Finland, )

title: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the Trafa API
text: Please note rTrafa project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

what: Compose a data query string — compose_data_query
title: Compose a data query string — compose_data_query
text: Builds pipe-delimited query string used Trafa /api/data endpoint.

title: Compose a data query string — compose_data_query
code: compose_data_query(product, measure, ...)

title: Compose a data query string — compose_data_query
text: product Character: product code (e.g. "t10011"). measure Character: measure name (e.g. "itrfslut"). ... Named arguments name dimension value character vector filter values (e.g. ar = c("2023", "2024")).

title: Compose a data query string — compose_data_query
text: character string (e.g. "t10011|itrfslut|ar:2023,2024").

title: Compose a data query string — compose_data_query
code: compose_data_query("t10011", "itrfslut") #> [1] "t10011|itrfslut" compose_data_query("t10011", "itrfslut", ar = "2024") #> [1] "t10011|itrfslut|ar:2024" compose_data_query("t10011", "itrfslut", ar = c("2023", "2024"),                    drivm = c("102", "103")) #> [1] "t10011|itrfslut|ar:2023,2024|drivm:102,103"

what: Compose a structure query string — compose_structure_query
title: Compose a structure query string — compose_structure_query
text: Builds pipe-delimited query string used Trafa /api/structure endpoint.

title: Compose a structure query string — compose_structure_query
code: compose_structure_query(product = NULL, ...)

title: Compose a structure query string — compose_structure_query
text: product Character: product code (e.g. "t10011"). ... Additional dimension names include query. bare names (filtered), used progressive structure discovery.

title: Compose a structure query string — compose_structure_query
text: character string (e.g. "t10011|ar|drivm"), empty string product NULL.

title: Compose a structure query string — compose_structure_query
code: compose_structure_query("t10011") #> [1] "t10011" compose_structure_query("t10011", "itrfslut", "ar") #> [1] "t10011|itrfslut|ar"

what: Generate a source caption for plots — data_legend
title: Generate a source caption for plots — data_legend
text: Builds human-readable source attribution string data tibble returned get_data(). string includes product measure along human-readable descriptions, suitable use caption ggplot2::labs().

title: Generate a source caption for plots — data_legend
code: data_legend(data_df, lang = NULL, omit_varname = FALSE, omit_desc = FALSE)

title: Generate a source caption for plots — data_legend
text: data_df tibble returned get_data(). lang Language caption text: "SV" (Swedish, default) "EN" (English). Defaults getOption("rTrafa.lang", "SV"). Note product/measure labels returned API default language regardless setting. omit_varname Logical. TRUE, omit variable codes (parenthesised IDs like t10011 itrfslut). omit_desc Logical. TRUE, omit human-readable descriptions show codes.

title: Generate a source caption for plots — data_legend
text: single character string suitable plot captions.

title: Generate a source caption for plots — data_legend
text: default caption shows description code, e.g. "Källa: Trafa; produkt: Bussar (t10011); mått: Antal trafik (itrfslut)". Use omit_varname drop codes omit_desc drop descriptions. Product measure descriptions looked via get_products() get_measures() (cached disk).

title: Generate a source caption for plots — data_legend
code: # \donttest{ if (trafa_available()) {   d <- get_data("t10011", "itrfslut", ar = "2024")   data_legend(d)   data_legend(d, lang = "EN")   data_legend(d, omit_varname = TRUE)   data_legend(d, omit_desc = TRUE) }# } #> [1] "Källa: Trafa; produkt: t10011; mått: itrfslut"

what: Fetch data from the Trafa API — get_data
title: Fetch data from the Trafa API — get_data
text: core function downloading statistical data. Dimension filters passed named arguments via ..., via prepared query object prepare_query().

title: Fetch data from the Trafa API — get_data
code: get_data(   product,   measure,   ...,   query = NULL,   lang = NULL,   simplify = TRUE,   cache = FALSE,   cache_location = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fetch data from the Trafa API — get_data
text: product Character: product code (e.g. "t10011"). Ignored query provided. measure Character: measure name (e.g. "itrfslut"). Ignored query provided. ... Dimension filters named arguments. name dimension name, value character vector filter values. Unspecified dimensions return values. Ignored query provided. query <trafa_query> object prepare_query(). provided, product, measure, ... taken query object. lang Language code: "SV" "EN". simplify Add human-readable label columns alongside codes. cache Logical. TRUE cache_location points SQLite file (nxt_handle nordstatExtras), data cached cell granularity database. Supports concurrent multi-process read/write cross-query cell reuse. Requires nordstatExtras. cache_location Either path .sqlite file nxt_handle returned nordstatExtras::nxt_open(). Ignored unless cache = TRUE. verbose Print request details.

title: Fetch data from the Trafa API — get_data
text: tibble data. Dimension columns use dimension name; simplify = TRUE, additional {name}_label columns added. Measure values column named measure.

title: Fetch data from the Trafa API — get_data
code: # \donttest{ if (trafa_available()) {   # Direct fetch   get_data("t10011", "itrfslut", ar = "2024")    # With filters   get_data("t10011", "itrfslut",     ar = c("2023", "2024"),     drivm = c("102", "103"))    # From a prepared query   q <- prepare_query("t10011", "itrfslut", ar = "2024")   get_data(query = q) }# } #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   ar    ar_label itrfslut #>   <chr> <chr>       <dbl> #> 1 2024  2024        14178

what: Get dimensions (filter variables) for a product — get_dimensions
title: Get dimensions (filter variables) for a product — get_dimensions
text: Retrieves available dimensions Trafa product. Dimensions categorical variables can filter fetching data get_data() — example year, fuel type, owner category.

title: Get dimensions (filter variables) for a product — get_dimensions
code: get_dimensions(   product,   measure = NULL,   only_valid = TRUE,   lang = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   cache_location = trafa_cache_dir,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Get dimensions (filter variables) for a product — get_dimensions
text: product Character: product code (e.g. "t10011"). measure Character vector one measure names. provided, dimensions valid measure(s) returned (unless only_valid = FALSE). Passing several measures restricts result dimensions valid . only_valid Logical. measure provided only_valid = TRUE (default), dimensions option = FALSE excluded. Set FALSE see dimensions validity status. lang Language code: "SV" "EN". cache Logical, cache results locally. cache_location Cache directory. Defaults trafa_cache_dir(). verbose Print request details.

title: Get dimensions (filter variables) for a product — get_dimensions
text: tibble columns: product, name, label, data_type, option, description, hierarchy, n_values, values. values column contains nested tibbles columns name, label, type ("value" regular dimension values, "filter" API filter shortcuts like "senaste" / latest).

title: Get dimensions (filter variables) for a product — get_dimensions
text: Dimensions belong hierarchy (e.g. "Ägarkategori" "Ägare" hierarchy) included hierarchy noted hierarchy column. Hierarchies queryable — child dimensions . measure provided, API validates dimensions compatible measure. Invalid dimensions excluded default (controlled only_valid). measure can also vector several measure names. case, API returns intersection: dimensions valid requested measures. useful planning query mixes several measures want know dimensions can safely filter .

title: Get dimensions (filter variables) for a product — get_dimensions
code: # \donttest{ if (trafa_available()) {   # All dimensions   get_dimensions("t10011") |> dimension_describe()    # Validated against a specific measure   get_dimensions("t10011", measure = "itrfslut")    # Validated against several measures (intersection)   get_dimensions("t10011", measure = c("itrfslut", "nyregunder"))    # Inspect values for a dimension   get_dimensions("t10011") |> dimension_values("drivm") }# } #> ── ar (År) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Data type: Time #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (25): 2001 = 2001, 2002 = 2002, 2003 = 2003, 2004 = 2004, 2005 = 2005 ... and 20 more #>   Filters: senaste = Senaste, forra = Föregående #>  #> ── avregform (Avregistreringsorsak) ─────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 20 = Utförda ur landet, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── dimpo (Direkt import) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 10 = Direkt import, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── leasing (Leasing) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (2): 30 = Leasade, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── bussklass (Bussklass) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Description: Bussklasser enligt föreskrift nr 107 UNECE #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (7): 1 = A, 2 = B, 3 = I, 4 = II, 5 = III ... and 2 more #>  #> ── drivm (Drivmedel) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (10): 101 = Bensin, 102 = Diesel, 103 = El, 104 = Elhybrid, 105 = Laddhybrid ... and 5 more #>  #> ── pass (Antal passagerare) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (12): 101 = – 20, 102 = 21 – 40, 103 = 41 – 50, 104 = 51 – 60, 105 = 61 – 70 ... and 7 more #>  #> ── agarkat (Ägarkategori)  [agare] ──────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (3): 10 = Fysisk person, 20 = Juridisk person, t1 = Totalt #>  #> ── tillst (Tillstånd)  [agare] ──────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Selectable: Yes  #>   Values (3): 1 = Yrkesmässig trafik, 2 = Firmabilstrafik, t1 = Totalt #>  #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    name  label      type  #>    <chr> <chr>      <chr> #>  1 101   Bensin     value #>  2 102   Diesel     value #>  3 103   El Totalt     value

what: Get measures (KPIs) for a product — get_measures
title: Get measures (KPIs) for a product — get_measures
text: Retrieves available measures Trafa product. measure represents specific statistic (KPI) can queried get_data(). product typically several measures — example, "Bussar" (t10011) measures vehicles traffic, deregistered, newly registered, etc.

title: Get measures (KPIs) for a product — get_measures
code: get_measures(   product,   lang = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   cache_location = trafa_cache_dir,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Get measures (KPIs) for a product — get_measures
text: product Character: product code (e.g. "t10011"). lang Language code: "SV" "EN". cache Logical, cache results locally. cache_location Cache directory. Defaults trafa_cache_dir(). verbose Print request details.

title: Get measures (KPIs) for a product — get_measures
text: tibble columns: product, name, label, description, id, unique_id, option, active_from, data_type, parent_name.

text: product Character: product code (e.g. "t10011"). measure Character: measure name (e.g. "itrfslut"). ... Ignored. lang Language code: "SV" "EN". validate Logical. TRUE (default), validates dimension compatibility API structure. verbose Print request details. x <trafa_query> object.

title: Prepare a data query with progressive validation — prepare_query
text: <trafa_query> object. Pass get_data() via query.

title: Prepare a data query with progressive validation — prepare_query

title: Print human-readable product summaries — product_describe
code: # \donttest{ if (trafa_available()) {   get_products() |> product_search("buss") |> product_describe() }# } #> ── t10011: Bussar ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Active from: 2019-01-10T10:17:00  #>  #> ── t10091: Bussar ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Description: Bussar #>  #> Definitioner-> Bussklass: För fordon som är inrättade för befordran av fler än 22 passagerare utöver föraren finns följande fordonsklasser: Klass I – Fordon som tillverkats med utrymmen för ståplatspassagerare för att medge frekventa förflyttningar av passagerare. Klass II – Fordon som huvudsakligen tillverkats för befordran av sittplatspassagerare och som är utformade för att medge befordran av ståplatspassagerare i mittgången och/eller i ett utrymme som inte är större än att det utrymme som upptas för två dubbelsäten. Klass III – Fordon som uteslutande tillverkats för befordran av sittplatspassagerare. För fordon som är inrättande för befordran av högst 22 passagerare utöver föraren finns följande fordonsklasser: Klass A – Fordon utformade för befordran av ståplatspassagerare. Ett fordon i denna klass är utrustat med säten och ska ha utrymme för ståplatspassagerare. Klass B – Fordon som inte är utformade för befordran av ståplatspassagerare. Ett fordon i denna klass saknar utrymme för ståplatspassagerare. #>   Active from: 2019-04-29T11:00:00  #>  #> ── t10021: Bussar ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Active from: 2020-05-08T12:59:00  #>

what: Check if a product is a data-bearing leaf or an empty container — product_has_data
title: Check if a product is a data-bearing leaf or an empty container — product_has_data
text: Trafa API model parent-child relationships products explicitly. However, products (e.g. "Fordon på väg", t10010) dimensions measures structure return data rows — act organizational containers. function checks whether product actual data inspecting structure dimension/measure items whose parent_name matches product code, verifying whether data endpoint returns rows.

title: Check if a product is a data-bearing leaf or an empty container — product_has_data
code: product_has_data(   product,   lang = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   cache_location = trafa_cache_dir,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Check if a product is a data-bearing leaf or an empty container — product_has_data
text: product Character: product code. lang, cache, cache_location, verbose Standard rTrafa args.

title: Check if a product is a data-bearing leaf or an empty container — product_has_data
text: Logical: TRUE product's data endpoint returns rows, FALSE appears empty container.

title: Check if a product is a data-bearing leaf or an empty container — product_has_data
text: Note: product container, related "sub-products" discovered programmatically via API. Use product_search() product catalogue find products similar names (e.g. product_search(get_products(), "fordon")).

title: Check if a product is a data-bearing leaf or an empty container — product_has_data
code: # \donttest{ if (trafa_available()) {   product_has_data("t10011")   # TRUE — Bussar has data   product_has_data("t10010")   # FALSE — container, no data rows }# } #> Warning: No data rows in Trafa API response. #> [1] FALSE

what: rTrafa: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the 'Trafa' API — rTrafa-package
title: rTrafa: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the 'Trafa' API — rTrafa-package
text: pipe-friendly R client Trafa statistical API (api.trafa.se). Provides search--fetch workflow discovering downloading Swedish transport infrastructure data using consistent tibble-based interface.

title: rTrafa: Access Swedish Transport Statistics from the 'Trafa' API — rTrafa-package
text: Maintainer: Love Hansson love.hansson@gmail.com [copyright holder]

what: Check if the Trafa API is available — trafa_available
title: Check if the Trafa API is available — trafa_available
text: Performs lightweight HTTP check verify Trafa API reachable. primarily useful guarding examples tests.

title: Check if the Trafa API is available — trafa_available
code: trafa_available()

title: Check if the Trafa API is available — trafa_available
text: TRUE API responds within 5 seconds, FALSE otherwise.

title: Check if the Trafa API is available — trafa_available
code: trafa_available() #> [1] TRUE

what: Get the persistent rTrafa cache directory — trafa_cache_dir
title: Get the persistent rTrafa cache directory — trafa_cache_dir
text: Returns path user-level cache directory rTrafa, creating exist. Uses tools::R_user_dir() cache survives across R sessions.

title: Get the persistent rTrafa cache directory — trafa_cache_dir
code: trafa_cache_dir()

title: Get the persistent rTrafa cache directory — trafa_cache_dir
text: single character string (directory path).

title: Get the persistent rTrafa cache directory — trafa_cache_dir
code: trafa_cache_dir() #> [1] "/home/runner/.cache/R/rTrafa"

title: Clear rTrafa cache files — trafa_clear_cache
text: Removes cached API responses stored default specified location.

title: Clear rTrafa cache files — trafa_clear_cache
code: trafa_clear_cache(entity = NULL, cache_location = trafa_cache_dir())

title: Clear rTrafa cache files — trafa_clear_cache
text: entity Character entity clear (e.g. "products", "structure"), NULL (default) clear rTrafa cache files. cache_location Directory clear. Defaults trafa_cache_dir().

title: Clear rTrafa cache files — trafa_clear_cache
text: invisible(NULL)

title: Clear rTrafa cache files — trafa_clear_cache
code: # \donttest{ if (trafa_available()) {   trafa_clear_cache()   trafa_clear_cache(entity = "products") }# } #> Removed 4 cached file(s). #> No rTrafa cache files found.

title: rTrafa 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.

title: rTrafa 0.1.0
text: API connection: trafa_available() performs lightweight connectivity check, used guard examples tests. Product discovery: get_products() lists statistical products (datasets) available Trafa API. Structure inspection: get_measures() get_dimensions() retrieve measures (KPIs) filterable dimensions given product, including hierarchy metadata dimension validation. Data retrieval: get_data() downloads data using pipe-delimited query syntax, automatic parsing optional simplification (human-readable _label columns alongside raw codes). Query workflow: prepare_query() validates selections structure endpoint hitting data endpoint; compose_structure_query() compose_data_query() expose programmatic access raw URL builders. Entity operations: entity type (product, measure, dimension) supports consistent family *_search(), *_describe(), *_extract_ids() / *_extract_names() *_minimize() helpers piped exploration. Filter shortcuts: dimension_values() surfaces Trafa’s server-side shortcuts (senaste, forra) alongside regular values, queries can always reach latest period without hardcoding years. Data helpers: data_minimize() drops monotonous columns; data_legend() generates source caption suitable ggplot2::labs(caption = ...), lang, omit_varname omit_desc arguments fine-grained control show. Persistent caching: trafa_cache_dir() trafa_clear_cache() manage cached API responses via tools::R_user_dir(). HTTP resilience: automatic retry exponential backoff transient errors. Offline-safe: examples vignettes guarded trafa_available() draw pre-cached API data stored R/sysdata.rda, package builds tests require network access.

title: rTrafa 0.1.0
text: Quick start vignette (-quickstart-rtrafa): five-step walk-product discovery plotted time series. Introduction vignette (introduction--rtrafa): covers four-level data model (product → measure → dimension → value), hierarchies, filter shortcuts, dimension validation, prepared queries, three worked ggplot2 examples. Vignette plots convert ar (year) column Date plotting use scale_x_date() axis breaks land whole years — pattern sibling packages rKolada pixieweb share. README vignettes cross-link sibling packages rKolada (Swedish municipal regional KPIs) pixieweb (PX-Web APIs Nordic statistics agencies).
